LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT HYUNDAI MOTORS AMERICA'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF IMPLIED WARRANTY AND PARAGRAPH 31 OF PLAINTIFF JEFFREY SUTTON'S COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR AS MOOT IN LIGHT OF THE RULING ON THE DEMURRER. JUDGE: STEPHEN MURPHY; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF OCT-25-2017 CONTINUED TO JAN-24-2018 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

DECLARATION OF DANIEL KALINOWSKI IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFFS REPLY TO DEFENDANTS OPPOSITION TO PLAINTIFFS MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER DISCOVERY RESPONSES FROM DEFENDANT AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS - REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION (TRANSACTION ID # 100026474) FILED BY PLAINTIFF SUTTON, JEFFREY
MINI MINUTES FOR NOV-27-2017 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT HYUNDAI MOTOR AMERICA'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF THE IMPLIED WARRANTY IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF JEFFREY SUTTON IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE ENTIRETY OF THAT CAUSE OF ACTION. SINCE IT APPEARS THAT IT IS NOT REASONABLY POSSIBLE FOR MR. SUTTON TO ALLEGE A VIABLE CLAIM FOR IMPLIED WARRANTY OF MERCHANTABILITY, THE DEMURRER TO THAT CLAIM IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES F. HUNTER, CSR NO. 6576, E-MAIL: RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG (L&M302)

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF JEFFREY SUTTON'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION FROM DEFENDANT HYUNDAI MOTOR AMERICA, AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE, PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER. PROCEEDINGS REPORTED BY NANCY E. PRESANT-MCDONALD CSR	906 TEL NO. 510.757.8550; JUDGE: DOUGLAS ROBBINS; CLERK: JOHNNY SENGMANY (HK/302/JPT).

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JEFFREY SUTTON'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES, COSTS AND EXPENSES. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF JEFFREY SUTTON'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES, COSTS, AND EXPENSES IS GRANTED IN PART. MR. SUTTON IS ENTITLED TO $19,855.33, COMPRISED OF $18,307.60 IN ATTORNEY'S FEES AND $1,547.73 IN COSTS. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR DETAILS) PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-20-2019 Off Calendar

Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion & Motion For Attorney Fees, Costs And Expenses
Off Calendar Dec-05-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Deepak Devabose, Esq. 310-552-2250 of Knight Law Group for Plaintiff.
Kainoa Aliviado, Esq. 424-297-3103 of Theta Law Firm for Defendant Hyundai Motor America through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares his views. Plaintiff argues portion worked. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF JEFFREY SUTTON's Motion For Attorney Fees, Costs And Expenses.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Plaintiff Jeffrey Sutton's motion for attorney fees, costs, and expenses is granted in part. Mr. Sutton is entitled to $19,855.33, comprised of $18,307.60 in attorney's fees and $1,547.73 in costs. In the court's ever-evolving effort to determine a reasonable fees award in a lemon law case, after review of the papers filed by the parties and court's own experience, the court has determined that the hourly rates and time claimed by Mr. Sutton's counsel are excessive for a case of this kind. Moreover, for a simple case nine different timekeepers is excessive. The high end of a reasonable range of rates and time spent is no more than 80% of the asserted hourly rates and time claimed by Mr. Sutton's counsel. Therefore, the court reduced the fees sought by 20%. The court further reduced the fees sought by eliminating all time claimed for services performed from January 26, 2018 to February 6, 2018 by the Altman firm and from January 27, 2018 to February 19, 2018 by the Knight firm because those services were performed after Mr. Sutton's counsel received and had time to review the second 998 offer that Mr. Sutton eventually accepted and the date that the offer was accepted. Services performed during that time only served to increase the amount of fees and costs sought by Mr. Sutton without any advantage to him in the case. No multiplier is awarded because there is no need for one due to contingent risk, delayed payment or any other reason supporting a multiplier. This was a simple case that proceeded in a standard way in a high volume repetitive lemon law claim practice. Costs claimed for the deposition of Mr. Sutton are not awarded due to the lack of need for that deposition which took place after Mr. Sutton had an opportunity to review and discuss the second 998 offer and before he accepted that offer. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Plaintiff to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-08-2017 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Kainoa k. Aliviado of the law offices of Theta Law Firm LLP (424-297-3103) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

No other appearance is made in this matter

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT HYUNDAI MOTOR AMERICA's DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to the second cause of action for breach of the implied warranty in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Jeffrey Sutton is sustained without leave to amend as to the entirety of that cause of action. As was done on the prior demurrer order, the second cause of action will be subdivided into separate claims of implied warranty of fitness and implied warranty of merchantability. The implied warranty of fitness claim fails because a demurrer to that claim was previously sustained without leave to amend and nothing has changed that requires or supports a different result. The implied warranty of merchantability claim fails because Mr. Sutton was informed by the prior demurrer order precisely what he needed to do to allege a viable claim and, instead of doing so, he used evasive language, thereby strongly suggesting that he is not able to comply with the guidance provided in the prior order. Since it appears that it is not reasonably possible for Mr. Sutton to allege a viable claim for implied warranty of merchantability, the demurrer to that claim is sustained without leave to amend. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR No. 6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org (L&M302)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-18-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JENNIFER REID OF KNIGHT LAW GROUP LLP, TEL. NO. TEL. NO. 1-310-552-2250 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;

RULING - THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT HYUNDAI MOTOR AMERICA'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT - Defendant Hyundai Motors America's motion to strike the second cause of action for breach of implied warranty and paragraph 31 of plaintiff Jeffrey Sutton's complaint is off calendar as moot in light of the ruling on the demurrer. Judge: Stephen Murphy; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 =302/SMM

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-18-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. JENNIFER REID OF KNIGHT LAW GROUP LLP, TEL. NO. TEL. NO. 1-310-552-2250 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. KAINOA K. ALIVIADO OF THE THETA LAW FIRM LLP, TEL. NO. 1-424-297-3103 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Hyundai Motors America's demurrer to the second cause of action in plaintiff Jeffrey Sutton's complaint is sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to the breach of implied warranty of merchantability claim and sustained without leave to amend as to the breach of implied warranty of fitness claim. The second cause of action alleges two distinct implied warranty claims which are treated separately for purposes of this demurrer. Preliminarily, although Hyundai raised a statute of limitations issue in its notice of demurrer, it was not addressed in the memorandum of points and authorities and is therefore deemed waived for purposes of the instant demurrer only. Mr. Sutton has not adequately alleged a breach of implied warranty of merchantability claim because the complaint fails to state that Hyundai was the distributor or seller of the used car at issue in this case. Pursuant to Civil Code 1795.5, the implied warranty of merchantability applies to used goods so long as they are purchased in a sale in which the distributor or retail seller of the consumer good gave an express warranty. "It shall be the obligation of the distributor or retail seller making the express warranty with respect to the used consumer goods (and not the original manufacturer, distributor or retail seller making express warranties with respect to such goods when new) to maintain sufficient service and repair facilities within this state to carry out the terms of such express warranties." (Civil Code 1795.5, subd.(a).) If he can do so in good faith, Mr. Sutton is granted twenty days leave to amend his complaint to clarify whether Hyundai was the distributor or seller of the used car at issue. Mr. Sutton failed to adequately allege a breach of implied warranty of fitness claim, nor is there any reasonable possibility that he can do so. Mr. Webster has not adequately alleged the third through fifth elements in CACI 3211-that, at the time of purchase, defendant knew or had reason to know that plaintiff was relying on its skill and judgment to select or provide a vehicle that was suitable for that particular purpose; that plaintiff justifiably relied on defendant's skill and judgment; and that the vehicle was not suitable for the particular purpose. It does not appear reasonably possible that amendment would cure the defect because the "family or household purposes" alleged in paragraph 18 of the complaint is an ordinary purpose and not a particular purpose of the type contemplated by the Song-Beverly Act. (See American Suzuki Motor Corp. v. Superior Court (1995) 37 Cal.App.4th 1291, 1295, fn 2.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Stephen Murphy; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 =302/sm
